title: Why I Build with Next.js in 2026
content:
Next.js has become my go-to framework for almost every project. Here's why.

Server Components Changed Everything

With React Server Components, I can fetch data directly in my components without worrying about client-side waterfalls. The mental model is simple: if it needs the database, it's a server component. If it needs interactivity, it's a client component.

The File System Router

Having routes map to file structure means zero configuration routing. Want a dynamic route? Create a folder with brackets. Need a layout? Add a layout.js. It just works.

Performance Out of the Box

Automatic code splitting, image optimization, font optimization, and static generation — all without touching a config file. My Lighthouse scores jumped from 60s to 90s just by switching from a traditional React SPA.

The best framework is the one that gets out of your way and lets you build.

What I Use It For

  • Portfolio sites (like this one)
  • E-commerce stores with Stripe
  • Internal dashboards
  • API backends with route handlers

Next.js isn't perfect, but it's the closest thing to a full-stack framework that feels natural.
